title: Locking ring
abstract: A locking ring for a plastic container for fluids, comprising a U-shaped channel fitting over a rim and a lid of the container, and also comprising a novel guide below a sealing ridge, said locking ring having a pre-installed position on the rim and the lid, and in said pre-installed position said guide being balanced evenly and fitting snugly against an outer portion of the rim of the container, said pre-installed position, and so that the locking ring may be pressed onto the rim and the lid with less force than otherwise.
claims: Claims Claims We claim: 1. A locking ring for a plastic container for fluid, said locking ring comprising a U- shaped channel fitted for receiving a rim and a lid of a container, said U-shaped channel comprising an inner wall, an inner wall radius and an inner surface defining a first side of said U shaped channel, and an outer wall and an outer surface defining a second side of said U shaped channel, said outer wall being integral to a sealing ridge and said sealing ridge being integral to a guide, so that when the sealing ridge and the guide arc is positioned in a pre-installed position snugly adjacent to an outermost portion of the rim, then the locking ring may be pressed to an installed position in which the sealing ridge makes a first contacts ith a sidewall of the container and an the outermost portion of the rim, and the inner wail and the inner wall radius make a second also contact -at the lid, and the first and second contacts create creating a void between the U-shaped channel and the rim and the lid in the installed position. 2. The locking ring as in claim 1. wherein when atmospheric pressure is less than air pressure inside the container and the lid is forced upward to a loaded position, the sealing ridge presses against the outermost portion of the rim and the first side of said U shaped channel presses against the lids and holds the lid in place. 3. The locking ring as in claim 1 , wherein the shape of the locking ring is circular. 4. The locking ring as in claim 1 , wherein the shape of the cross section locking ring is rectangular with rounded comers. 5. The locking ring as in claim 1 , further comprising a first slope between an apex of the sealing ridge and a first point of the sealing ridge, and the first slope is straight between the first point and the apex.
